preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 41% to win, with Argentina trailing at 28%. The 13-percentage-point gap reflects a meaningful but not dominant edge for the favorite on a neutral ground. Spain's win probability exceeds Argentina's by roughly half as much again.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 31%, suggesting the teams are close enough in capability that a stalemate is nearly as likely as an Argentina victory. The spread across outcomes is moderately compressed—no single result dominates decisively—indicating a competitive match where multiple scenarios carry genuine probability.

Model and market are tightly aligned. Sportsbook consensus sits at 42% Spain, 32% draw, and 27% Argentina; Polymarket prices at 42%, 31%, and 26% respectively. All three sources cluster within 2 percentage points of each other across each outcome, suggesting broad agreement on the relative strengths in play.
